text_blocks.text: Port Phillip is often warmer than the surrounding
oceans and/or the land mass, particularly in spring and
autumn; this can set up a "bay effect" similar to the "lake
effect" seen in colder climates where showers are intensified
leeward of the bay. Relatively narrow streams of heavy
showers can often affect the same places (usually the
eastern suburbs) for an extended period, while the rest of
Melbourne and surrounds stays dry. Overall, Melbourne is,
owing to the rain shadow of the Otway Ranges, nonetheless
drier than average for southern Victoria. Within the city
and surrounds, however, rainfall varies widely, from around
425 millimetres (17 in) at Little River to 1,250 millimetries (49
in) on the eastern fringe at Gembrook. Melbourne receives
48.6 clear days annually. Dewpoint temperatures in the
summer range from 9.5 degC (49.1 degF) to 11.7 degC ($53.1
